title: Cyminosma pedunculata
intro: Cyminosma pedunculata, also known as Australian borneol, is a plant species native to Australia, belonging to the family Apiaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities, making it a potential treatment for various health issues such as wound healing and infections. Bioactive constituents of Cyminosma pedunculata include borneol, camphene, and limonene, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include essential oils, extracts, and infusions, which are used in traditional medicine to support immune function and skin health.
taxonomy: Cyminosma pedunculata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae. It belongs to the genus Cyminosma, which is a group of flowering plants found in Australia. Cyminosma pedunculata is further classified as a shrub, typically growing up to 1-2 meters in height. The plant has been classified as being of least concern on the IUCN Red List. Its classification is based on its botanical characteristics and distribution within its native range.
uses: Cyminosma pedunculata, a plant native to New Zealand, has been used in traditional Maori medicine for various purposes. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to treat wounds and skin conditions.
